commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `{ item: { oldText, newText } }` edit-entry unwrapping in edit argument preparation and direct edit execution, with wrapped and mixed edit regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +46. Total +95 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction: current main validates direct `oldText`/`newText` edit entries and does not unwrap `{ item: ... }` array entries before validation. No live provider/XML-RPC reproduction was run here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because automation cannot supply proof from the affected setup, and the current head also needs a small type-safe test adjustment.

target.title: fix(edit): unwrap XML-RPC {item: {oldText, newText}} edit entries
target.body:
## Summary

Adds XML-RPC-style `{ item: ... }` edit-entry unwrapping in edit argument
preparation and direct execution. Fixes #75152.

## Root Cause

XML-RPC array-of-structs wraps each element as `{item: {oldText, newText}}`
instead of the canonical `{oldText, newText}`. The edit tool did not unwrap
this transport format.

## Fix

- `prepareEditArguments()`: unwrap `{item: ...}` entries before validation
- `createEditToolDefinition()`: duplicate unwrap for direct execute() calls

## Proof — Tests pass with wrapped format

```
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12) — including both wrapper tests
```

### Test fixes (this commit)

The test data now actually exercises the wrapper format:
- `edits: [{ item: { oldText, newText } }]` for single wrapped entry
- `edits: [{ oldText, newText }, { item: { oldText, newText } }]` for mixed

Previously the tests used canonical entries that would pass even without the
unwrap logic.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
